author: Tertullian
book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II

lineage: It is Not Enough that God Know Us to Be Chaste: We Must Seem So Before Men. Especially in These Times of Persecution We Must Inure Our Bodies to the Hardships Which They May Not Improbably Be Called to Suffer.

title: It is Not Enough that God Know Us to Be Chaste: We Must Seem So Before Men. Especially in These Times of Persecution We Must Inure Our Bodies to the Hardships Which They May Not Improbably Be Called to Suffer.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Perhaps some (woman) will say: “To me it is not necessary to be approved by men; for I do not require the testimony of men: God is the inspector of the heart.”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: (That) we all know; provided, however, we remember what the same (God) has said through the apostle: “Let your probity appear before men.”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For what purpose, except that malice may have no access at all to you, or that you may be an example and testimony to the evil?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Else, what is (that): “Let your works shine?”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Why, moreover, does the Lord call us the light of the world; why has He compared us to a city built upon a mountain; if we do not shine in (the midst of) darkness, and stand eminent amid them who are sunk down?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: If you hide your lamp beneath a bushel, you must necessarily be left quite in darkness, and be run against by many.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: The things which make us luminaries of the world are these—our good works.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: What is good, moreover, provided it be true and full, loves not darkness: it joys in being seen, and exults over the very pointings which are made at it.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: To Christian modesty it is not enough to be so, but to seem so too.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For so great ought its plenitude to be, that it may flow out from the mind to the garb, and burst out from the conscience to the outward appearance; so that even from the outside it may gaze, as it were, upon its own furniture,—(a furniture) such as to be suited to retain faith as its inmate perpetually.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For such delicacies as tend by their softness and effeminacy to unman the manliness of faith are to be discarded.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Otherwise, I know not whether the wrist that has been wont to be surrounded with the palmleaf-like bracelet will endure till it grow into the numb hardness of its own chain!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: I know not whether the leg that has rejoiced in the anklet will suffer itself to be squeezed into the gyve!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: I fear the neck, beset with pearl and emerald nooses, will give no room to the broadsword!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Wherefore, blessed (sisters), let us meditate on hardships, and we shall not feel them; let us abandon luxuries, and we shall not regret them.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Let us stand ready to endure every violence, having nothing which we may fear to leave behind.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: It is these things which are the bonds which retard our hope.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Let us cast away earthly ornaments if we desire heavenly.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Love not gold; in which (one substance) are branded all the sins of the people of Israel.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: You ought to hate what ruined your fathers; what was adored by them who were forsaking God.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Even then (we find) gold is food for the fire.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: But Christians always, and now more than ever, pass their times not in gold but in iron: the stoles of martyrdom are (now) preparing: the angels who are to carry us are (now) being awaited!

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Do you go forth (to meet them) already arrayed in the cosmetics and ornaments of prophets and apostles; drawing your whiteness from simplicity, your ruddy hue from modesty; painting your eyes with bashfulness, and your mouth with silence; implanting in your ears the words of God; fitting on your necks the yoke of Christ.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Submit your head to your husbands, and you will be enough adorned.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Busy your hands with spinning; keep your feet at home; and you will “please” better than (by arraying yourselves) in gold.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Clothe yourselves with the silk of uprightness, the fine linen of holiness, the purple of modesty.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Thus painted, you will have God as your Lover!
